div.p: Critical sequence for x86 mode transition

Maximum safe delay after CR0.PE=1: 0 instructions The far jump MUST be the very next instruction after setting CR0.PE. The CPU is in an inconsistent state: paging logic is active but CS still contains a real-mode value. Any instruction fetch or memory access before the far jump has undefined behavior.

Valid: • 16-bit registers (AX, BX...) • Segment:offset addressing • 1MB addressable memory • BIOS interrupts (INT 10h, etc.) Invalid: • 32-bit registers • Memory above 1MB • Page tables • Privilege separation
Valid: • GDT defined in memory • GDTR points to GDT • Still in real mode! Invalid: • Cannot use GDT selectors yet • Still 16-bit operations • CR0.PE = 0
WARNING: Hybrid State! • Paging bit set in CR0 • CS still has real-mode value • Pipeline has 16-bit instructions CRITICAL: Must far jump IMMEDIATELY! Any delay = undefined behavior
Valid: • CS = 0x08 (kernel code sel) • 32-bit instruction fetch • GDT lookup for CS base/limit Invalid: • DS/ES/SS still real-mode • Cannot safely access data • Interrupts dangerous (IDT?)
Valid: • All seg regs = 0x10 • 32-bit EAX, EBX, ECX... • Full 4GB address space • Memory protection active Ready for: • IDT setup • Paging enable • Kernel C code
